tests.intent: Package metadata exposes exactly seven uniquely named, non-overloaded Section 5.4 procedures with exact argument direction, ordinal, and SQL types.
ORDS metadata exposes exactly DOOM_API and non-updatable PUBLIC_HEALTH while every base table, sequence, package helper, and state object remains unreachable.
Least-privilege grants permit only package execution and health selection to the REST principal and deny direct dynamic/static table privileges.
All seven procedures accept POST application/json on canonical AutoREST paths; GET, PUT, PATCH, DELETE and alternate/custom routes fail closed.
NEW_GAME validates skill and caps, creates a cryptographically random 32-lowercase-hex token, reclaims bounded expiry rows, and returns gzip canonical payload.
STEP enforces exact JSON envelope/keysets, integer/range rules, one to four consecutive commands, 64-KiB byte limit, ownership, expiry, and configured caps.
Exact terminal-batch retry returns identical cached compressed bytes without tic/event/RNG mutation; conflicting, older, gapped, and failed requests roll back wholly.
Two simultaneous STEP calls for one session serialize or reject one in-flight call without double execution, while independent sessions make forward progress.
SAVE_GAME and LOAD_GAME enforce session/slot ownership, stable lowercase state hash, atomic errors, and gzip payload whose restored canonical state matches the saved hash.
Replay range validation, opaque replay identity, bounded cursor advancement, terminal behavior, session isolation, and STEP_REPLAY gzip determinism are exact.
GET_ASSET serves only configured exact allowlist names with deterministic bytes and media types; traversal, case tricks, encoded separators, and unknown assets fail.
Every frame BLOB has gzip magic, decompresses to exact canonical JSON keys, 320 columns each covering 200 rows, valid audio tuples, and independent state/frame hashes.
PUBLIC_HEALTH is non-updatable and side-effect-free, reveals no secrets, reports readiness, and no permissive wildcard/null CORS policy is emitted or configured.
Static production audit rejects public helpers, overloads, custom handlers, table exposure, evaluator coupling, dynamic SQL, unsafe exception commits, and missing rollback mapping.
Focused semantic mutations, evaluator self-tests, approved ancestry pointers, fixture/reference hashes, and production-independent adversarial canaries all fail closed.
